nodes.en: The natural sciences concerned with the planet Earth — its solid interior and surface, oceans, atmosphere, and their history and dynamics. Sibling to Physics, Chemistry, Biology, and Astronomy; it groups geology, meteorology, oceanography, and the other disciplines that study Earth's materials, processes, and past.
nodes.cs: Přírodní vědy zabývající se planetou Zemí — jejím pevným nitrem a povrchem, oceány, atmosférou a jejich historií a dynamikou. Sourozenec fyziky, chemie, biologie a astronomie; sdružuje geologii, meteorologii, oceánografii a další obory studující materiály, procesy a minulost Země.
nodes.brief:
Earth Sciences is the group of natural sciences concerned with the planet Earth: its solid interior and surface, its oceans and atmosphere, and the history and dynamics that shaped them. It treats Earth as an integrated system in which rock, water, air and ice interact on timescales from seconds (an earthquake) to billions of years (the formation of a continent).

Earth Sciences sits beside [[01.05]] within the Natural Sciences: astronomy studies Earth's neighbors and the universe beyond, while Earth Sciences turns the same physical methods inward on the one planet its subjects can sample directly. Its subfields split along Earth's major components: [[01.04.01]] studies the solid Earth — rocks, minerals and the processes that build and erode them — [[01.04.02]] studies the atmosphere and the short-term weather it produces, and [[01.04.03]] studies the oceans and the circulation that links them to climate. Several subfields, notably seismology and geophysics, share their quantitative core with [[01.01]], applying physical laws of wave propagation and gravity to Earth's interior.

Earth Sciences also borders geology's sibling record-keepers — paleontology reads Earth's history in fossils, glaciology in ice cores — giving the field both a present-tense and a deep-time view of the same planet. For further reading, see the [USGS educational resources](https://www.usgs.gov/educational-resources) and the [Encyclopaedia Britannica entry on earth science](https://www.britannica.com/science/earth-science).

nodes.en: The study of the solid Earth — its rocks, minerals, structure, and the processes (tectonics, erosion, deposition) that shape the crust over time. Broadest of the Earth-science siblings; distinct from Mineralogy (individual minerals), Seismology (earthquakes), and Paleontology (fossils), which examine specific facets of the material Earth.
nodes.cs: Studium pevné Země — jejích hornin, minerálů, stavby a procesů (tektoniky, eroze, ukládání), jež v čase formují zemskou kůru. Nejširší z oborů věd o Zemi; odlišná od mineralogie (jednotlivé minerály), seismologie (zemětřesení) a paleontologie (zkameněliny), které zkoumají dílčí stránky hmotné Země.
nodes.brief:
Geology is the study of the solid Earth — its rocks, minerals, structures, and the tectonic, erosional, and depositional processes that reshape the crust over timescales from a single storm to hundreds of millions of years. It reconstructs Earth's history from the record preserved in strata, folds, faults, and mineral assemblages, and it underlies the practical search for groundwater, ores, and hydrocarbons.

Within [[01.04]], geology is the broadest of the solid-Earth disciplines, and several neighbors specialize in pieces of the picture it assembles: [[01.04.06]] studies the individual crystalline substances that compose geology's rocks, [[01.04.07]] follows the molten rock that geology's igneous processes leave behind, and [[01.04.08]] reads the elastic waves that reveal the deep structure geology can only infer from surface exposures. [[01.04.05]] draws on geology's stratigraphic record to date and contextualize the fossils it studies, making the two fields close historical collaborators.

For further orientation, see the US Geological Survey's [educational resources](https://www.usgs.gov/educational-resources) and the [Encyclopaedia Britannica entry on geology](https://www.britannica.com/science/geology).

nodes.name: Petrology
nodes.en: Studies the origin, composition, texture, and classification of rocks, tracing how melts, sediments, and pre-existing rocks transform into the three great rock families under distinct pressure-temperature regimes.
nodes.cs: Zkoumá původ, složení, strukturu a klasifikaci hornin a sleduje, jak se z tavenin, sedimentů a starších hornin za odlišných tlakově-teplotních režimů utvářejí tři velké skupiny hornin.

nodes.name: Igneous petrology
nodes.en: Examines rocks crystallized from magma or lava, linking mineral assemblage and texture to cooling rate and melt chemistry, from coarse plutonic granite to fine-grained volcanic basalt.
nodes.cs: Zkoumá horniny vykrystalizované z magmatu nebo lávy a spojuje minerální složení a strukturu s rychlostí chladnutí a chemismem taveniny, od hrubozrnného hlubinného granitu po jemnozrnný výlevný bazalt.
nodes.brief:
Examines rocks crystallized from magma or lava, linking mineral assemblage and texture to cooling rate and melt chemistry, from coarse plutonic granite to fine-grained volcanic basalt.

### Concepts
- **Bowen's reaction series** — The predictable sequence in which silicate minerals crystallize from a cooling magma, controlling the mineralogy of the resulting rock.
- **Fractional crystallization** — Progressive removal of early-formed crystals from a melt, driving the residual magma toward more silica-rich compositions.
- **Plutonic vs volcanic** — The distinction between slowly cooled intrusive rocks with large crystals and rapidly cooled extrusive rocks with fine or glassy textures.

nodes.name: Sedimentary petrology
nodes.en: Analyzes rocks formed by deposition, compaction, and cementation of weathered detritus, chemical precipitates, or organic material, reading grain size and composition as a record of transport and environment.
nodes.cs: Analyzuje horniny vzniklé usazováním, stlačením a stmelením zvětralého detritu, chemických sraženin či organického materiálu a čte velikost a složení zrn jako záznam transportu a prostředí.
nodes.brief:
Analyzes rocks formed by deposition, compaction, and cementation of weathered detritus, chemical precipitates, or organic material, reading grain size and composition as a record of transport and environment.

### Concepts
- **Clastic rocks** — Sedimentary rocks built from fragments of pre-existing rock, classified chiefly by grain size into conglomerate, sandstone, siltstone, and shale.
- **Diagenesis** — The physical and chemical changes that turn loose sediment into solid rock at low temperature and pressure after burial.
- **Chemical & biogenic sediments** — Rocks such as limestone or evaporites formed by precipitation from solution or accumulation of organic remains.

nodes.name: Metamorphic petrology
nodes.en: Studies rocks recrystallized in the solid state under elevated heat, pressure, and chemically active fluids, using index minerals and fabric to reconstruct the metamorphic grade and tectonic path.
nodes.cs: Zkoumá horniny překrystalované v pevném stavu za zvýšené teploty, tlaku a působení chemicky aktivních fluid a využívá indexové minerály a stavbu k rekonstrukci stupně metamorfózy a tektonické dráhy.
nodes.brief:
Studies rocks recrystallized in the solid state under elevated heat, pressure, and chemically active fluids, using index minerals and fabric to reconstruct the metamorphic grade and tectonic path.

### Concepts
- **Metamorphic facies** — A set of mineral assemblages that indicate a specific range of pressure and temperature during metamorphism.
- **Foliation** — The planar alignment of platy minerals produced by directed stress, defining textures such as slate, schist, and gneiss.
- **Contact vs regional metamorphism** — The contrast between localized heating around an intrusion and broad transformation driven by mountain-building tectonics.

nodes.name: Structural Geology & Tectonics
nodes.en: Describes how rocks deform and how lithospheric plates move, connecting outcrop-scale faults and folds to the global engine of plate tectonics that builds mountains and opens oceans.
nodes.cs: Popisuje, jak se horniny deformují a jak se pohybují litosférické desky, a propojuje zlomy a vrásy v měřítku odkryvu s globálním motorem deskové tektoniky, který vytváří pohoří a otevírá oceány.

nodes.name: Plate tectonics
nodes.en: The unifying theory that Earth's rigid outer shell is broken into plates that diverge, converge, and slide past one another, driven by mantle convection and slab pull.
nodes.cs: Sjednocující teorie, podle níž je pevný vnější obal Země rozlámán na desky, jež se od sebe vzdalují, sbíhají a posouvají podél sebe, poháněné konvekcí v plášti a tahem podsouvané desky.
nodes.brief:
The unifying theory that Earth's rigid outer shell is broken into plates that diverge, converge, and slide past one another, driven by mantle convection and slab pull.

### Concepts
- **Divergent boundary** — A plate margin where plates move apart and new lithosphere forms by seafloor spreading, as at mid-ocean ridges.
- **Subduction** — The sinking of one plate beneath another at a convergent margin, recycling lithosphere into the mantle and feeding volcanism.
- **Transform boundary** — A margin where plates slide horizontally past each other, conserving lithosphere and generating shallow earthquakes.
- **Wilson cycle** — The repeated opening and closing of ocean basins through rifting, spreading, subduction, and continental collision.

nodes.name: Faults & folds
nodes.en: The primary structures of brittle and ductile deformation, where fractures with displacement accommodate stress in cool rock and bends record flow in warmer, ductile conditions.
nodes.cs: Základní struktury křehké a duktilní deformace, kde zlomy s posunem uvolňují napětí v chladných horninách a vrásy zaznamenávají tečení v teplejších, duktilních podmínkách.
nodes.brief:
The primary structures of brittle and ductile deformation, where fractures with displacement accommodate stress in cool rock and bends record flow in warmer, ductile conditions.

### Concepts
- **Normal & reverse faults** — Dip-slip fractures produced by extension and compression respectively, offsetting rock along an inclined plane.
- **Anticline & syncline** — Convex-up and concave-up folds that arch or trough layered rock under compression.
- **Stress & strain** — The force per area applied to rock and the resulting change in shape or volume that it undergoes.

nodes.name: Orogeny
nodes.en: The mountain-building process arising from plate convergence, thickening and deforming the crust through folding, thrusting, and magmatism over tens of millions of years.
nodes.cs: Proces vrásnění vznikající při konvergenci desek, jenž zesiluje a deformuje kůru vrásněním, přesouváním a magmatismem po desítky milionů let.
nodes.brief:
The mountain-building process arising from plate convergence, thickening and deforming the crust through folding, thrusting, and magmatism over tens of millions of years.

### Concepts
- **Collisional orogeny** — Mountain building where two continents collide, as in the Himalaya raised by the India-Eurasia collision.
- **Nappe** — A large body of rock thrust many kilometers over underlying units along a low-angle fault during intense compression.

nodes.name: Stratigraphy & Sedimentology
nodes.en: Orders layered rocks in time and space and reconstructs the depositional environments that produced them, providing the temporal framework for reading Earth history from strata.
nodes.cs: Uspořádává vrstevnaté horniny v čase a prostoru a rekonstruuje usazovací prostředí, jež je vytvořilo, čímž poskytuje časový rámec pro čtení dějin Země z vrstev.

nodes.name: Stratigraphic principles
nodes.en: The foundational laws by which relative age is inferred from layered rocks, ordering events without numerical dates.
nodes.cs: Základní zákony, podle nichž se z vrstevnatých hornin odvozuje relativní stáří a řadí se události bez číselného datování.
nodes.brief:
The foundational laws by which relative age is inferred from layered rocks, ordering events without numerical dates.

### Concepts
- **Superposition** — In an undisturbed sequence, younger layers lie above older ones.
- **Original horizontality** — Sediments are deposited in nearly horizontal layers, so tilting indicates later deformation.
- **Cross-cutting relationships** — A feature that cuts across rock is younger than the rock it disrupts.

nodes.name: Sedimentary environments
nodes.en: The distinct settings—fluvial, deltaic, marine, aeolian, glacial—each imprinting characteristic grain textures, structures, and fossils onto accumulating sediment.
nodes.cs: Odlišná prostředí – říční, deltová, mořská, eolická, ledovcová – z nichž každé vtiskuje hromadícímu se sedimentu charakteristickou zrnitost, struktury a zkameněliny.
nodes.brief:
The distinct settings—fluvial, deltaic, marine, aeolian, glacial—each imprinting characteristic grain textures, structures, and fossils onto accumulating sediment.

### Concepts
- **Facies** — A body of sediment with distinctive characteristics reflecting the conditions under which it formed.
- **Sedimentary structures** — Features such as cross-bedding and ripple marks that record the flow conditions during deposition.

nodes.name: Sequence stratigraphy
nodes.en: Interprets stratal packages bounded by unconformities as the product of changing sea level and sediment supply, linking depositional geometry to base-level cycles.
nodes.cs: Vykládá vrstevní soubory ohraničené diskordancemi jako výsledek měnící se hladiny moře a přísunu sedimentu a spojuje usazovací geometrii s cykly bazální úrovně.
nodes.brief:
Interprets stratal packages bounded by unconformities as the product of changing sea level and sediment supply, linking depositional geometry to base-level cycles.

### Concepts
- **Unconformity** — A buried erosional or non-depositional surface representing a gap in the geologic record.
- **Transgression & regression** — The landward and seaward migration of the shoreline as relative sea level rises or falls.

nodes.name: Geomorphology
nodes.en: Studies landforms and the surface processes—weathering, erosion, transport, and deposition by water, wind, and ice—that sculpt the Earth's evolving topography.
nodes.cs: Zkoumá tvary zemského povrchu a povrchové procesy – zvětrávání, erozi, transport a ukládání vodou, větrem a ledem – které utvářejí vyvíjející se reliéf Země.

nodes.name: Weathering & erosion
nodes.en: The in-place breakdown of rock by mechanical and chemical processes and the subsequent removal of loosened material by gravity, water, wind, or ice.
nodes.cs: Rozpad horniny na místě mechanickými a chemickými pochody a následné odnášení uvolněného materiálu gravitací, vodou, větrem či ledem.
nodes.brief:
The in-place breakdown of rock by mechanical and chemical processes and the subsequent removal of loosened material by gravity, water, wind, or ice.

### Concepts
- **Physical weathering** — Mechanical disintegration of rock through frost wedging, thermal stress, and abrasion without chemical change.
- **Chemical weathering** — Decomposition of minerals by reactions such as hydrolysis, oxidation, and dissolution, altering rock composition.
- **Mass wasting** — The downslope movement of rock and soil under gravity, ranging from slow creep to rapid landslides.

nodes.name: Fluvial landforms
nodes.en: Features shaped by running water, from erosional valleys and canyons to depositional floodplains, deltas, and alluvial fans that dominate most continental surfaces.
nodes.cs: Tvary formované tekoucí vodou, od erozních údolí a kaňonů po akumulační nivy, delty a náplavové kužely, které převládají na většině pevninského povrchu.
nodes.brief:
Features shaped by running water, from erosional valleys and canyons to depositional floodplains, deltas, and alluvial fans that dominate most continental surfaces.

### Concepts
- **Meander** — A sinuous bend of a river channel that migrates laterally by eroding its outer bank and depositing on the inner.
- **Drainage basin** — The land area from which all precipitation drains to a common outlet through a network of channels.

nodes.name: Aeolian & karst landscapes
nodes.en: Distinctive terrains produced by wind transport in arid regions and by dissolution of soluble bedrock, yielding dunes, loess, caves, and sinkholes.
nodes.cs: Osobité krajiny vytvořené transportem větrem v suchých oblastech a rozpouštěním rozpustného podloží, dávající vzniknout dunám, spraši, jeskyním a závrtům.
nodes.brief:
Distinctive terrains produced by wind transport in arid regions and by dissolution of soluble bedrock, yielding dunes, loess, caves, and sinkholes.

### Concepts
- **Dune** — A mound or ridge of wind-blown sand whose shape reflects sand supply and prevailing wind direction.
- **Karst** — Terrain formed by dissolution of limestone or other soluble rock, characterized by caves, sinkholes, and underground drainage.

nodes.name: Geochronology & Earth History
nodes.en: Establishes the absolute and relative ages of rocks and events, assembling them into the geologic time scale that chronicles 4.6 billion years of planetary evolution.
nodes.cs: Stanovuje absolutní a relativní stáří hornin a událostí a skládá je do geologické časové stupnice, jež zaznamenává 4,6 miliardy let vývoje planety.

nodes.name: Radiometric dating
nodes.en: Determines numerical ages by measuring the decay of naturally occurring radioactive isotopes, whose constant half-lives act as precise geological clocks.
nodes.cs: Určuje číselné stáří měřením rozpadu přirozeně se vyskytujících radioaktivních izotopů, jejichž konstantní poločasy fungují jako přesné geologické hodiny.
nodes.brief:
Determines numerical ages by measuring the decay of naturally occurring radioactive isotopes, whose constant half-lives act as precise geological clocks.

### Concepts
- **Half-life** — The time required for half of a radioactive isotope in a sample to decay, fixed for each isotope.
- **Parent-daughter ratio** — The measured proportion of an original isotope to its decay product, used to calculate elapsed time.
- **Radiocarbon dating** — The use of carbon-14 decay to date organic material up to about fifty thousand years old.

nodes.name: Geologic time scale
nodes.en: The hierarchical division of Earth history into eons, eras, periods, and epochs, defined by major biological and physical events preserved in the rock record.
nodes.cs: Hierarchické členění dějin Země na eony, éry, periody a epochy, vymezené velkými biologickými a fyzikálními událostmi zachovanými v horninovém záznamu.
nodes.brief:
The hierarchical division of Earth history into eons, eras, periods, and epochs, defined by major biological and physical events preserved in the rock record.

### Concepts
- **Eon** — The largest division of geologic time, such as the Phanerozoic spanning abundant complex life.
- **Golden spike (GSSP)** — A globally agreed reference point in a rock section that defines the lower boundary of a time unit.

nodes.name: Economic & Engineering Geology
nodes.en: Applies geological knowledge to locating mineral and energy resources and to assessing ground conditions for construction and natural-hazard mitigation.
nodes.cs: Uplatňuje geologické poznatky při vyhledávání nerostných a energetických surovin a při posuzování základových poměrů pro stavebnictví a zmírňování přírodních rizik.

nodes.name: Ore deposits
nodes.en: Concentrations of economically valuable minerals formed by hydrothermal, magmatic, or sedimentary processes that enrich metals far above crustal averages.
nodes.cs: Nahromadění ekonomicky cenných nerostů vzniklá hydrotermálními, magmatickými či sedimentárními procesy, jež obohacují kovy daleko nad průměr zemské kůry.
nodes.brief:
Concentrations of economically valuable minerals formed by hydrothermal, magmatic, or sedimentary processes that enrich metals far above crustal averages.

### Concepts
- **Hydrothermal deposit** — An ore body precipitated from hot mineral-bearing fluids circulating through fractures in rock.
- **Placer deposit** — A concentration of dense resistant minerals such as gold, sorted and accumulated by flowing water.

nodes.name: Petroleum geology
nodes.en: The study of the source, migration, and trapping of hydrocarbons, integrating organic-rich source rocks, permeable reservoirs, and impermeable seals into productive systems.
nodes.cs: Nauka o zdroji, migraci a zachycování uhlovodíků, jež spojuje organicky bohaté zdrojové horniny, propustné kolektory a nepropustné izolátory do produktivních systémů.
nodes.brief:
The study of the source, migration, and trapping of hydrocarbons, integrating organic-rich source rocks, permeable reservoirs, and impermeable seals into productive systems.

### Concepts
- **Source rock** — An organic-rich sediment that generates hydrocarbons when heated during burial.
- **Reservoir & trap** — A porous rock that stores hydrocarbons and the geometric configuration that prevents their escape.

nodes.name: Geotechnical & geohazards
nodes.en: Evaluates the mechanical behavior of soil and rock for engineering, and identifies hazards such as landslides, subsidence, and swelling ground that threaten infrastructure.
nodes.cs: Hodnotí mechanické chování zemin a hornin pro inženýrské stavby a rozpoznává rizika jako sesuvy, poklesy a bobtnající podloží, jež ohrožují infrastrukturu.
nodes.brief:
Evaluates the mechanical behavior of soil and rock for engineering, and identifies hazards such as landslides, subsidence, and swelling ground that threaten infrastructure.

### Concepts
- **Slope stability** — The resistance of an inclined mass of soil or rock to failure by sliding under gravity.
- **Bearing capacity** — The maximum load per area that a soil or rock foundation can support without failure.

nodes.en: The study of the atmosphere and its short-term behavior — weather, pressure systems, precipitation, winds, and forecasting. Distinguished from Climatology, which studies long-term atmospheric statistics and change: Meteorology addresses the day-to-day and hour-to-hour state of the atmosphere.
nodes.cs: Studium atmosféry a jejího krátkodobého chování — počasí, tlakových systémů, srážek, větrů a předpovídání. Odlišná od klimatologie, jež studuje dlouhodobé statistiky a změny atmosféry: meteorologie se zabývá stavem atmosféry ze dne na den a z hodiny na hodinu.
nodes.brief:
Meteorology is the study of the atmosphere's short-term behavior — pressure systems, fronts, precipitation, winds, and the physical processes that drive day-to-day and hour-to-hour weather. It combines observational networks, radar and satellite data, and numerical models to explain current conditions and forecast how they will evolve over the coming hours to weeks.

Within [[01.04]], meteorology is distinguished from [[01.04.04]] by timescale: where climatology characterizes the long-term statistics and drivers of atmospheric regimes, meteorology addresses the atmosphere's immediate state. It exchanges energy and moisture constantly with [[01.04.03]], whose currents and sea-surface temperatures set the boundary conditions for much of the weather meteorology forecasts, and it shares its water-cycle accounting with [[01.04.09]], which tracks precipitation once it reaches the land surface.

For further reading, see the [National Weather Service's JetStream](https://www.noaa.gov/jetstream) online weather school and the World Meteorological Organization's [overview of meteorology](https://public.wmo.int/en).

nodes.name: Atmospheric Structure & Composition
nodes.en: Describes the vertical layering of the atmosphere and its gaseous makeup, establishing the physical stage on which all weather and radiative processes unfold.
nodes.cs: Popisuje vertikální vrstvení atmosféry a její plynné složení a vymezuje tak fyzikální prostor, v němž probíhají veškeré počasí a zářivé procesy.

nodes.name: Layers of the atmosphere
nodes.en: The concentric shells—troposphere, stratosphere, mesosphere, thermosphere—distinguished by their temperature gradients and the physical processes that dominate each.
nodes.cs: Soustředné vrstvy – troposféra, stratosféra, mezosféra, termosféra – odlišené svými teplotními gradienty a fyzikálními procesy, jež v každé z nich převládají.
nodes.brief:
The concentric shells—troposphere, stratosphere, mesosphere, thermosphere—distinguished by their temperature gradients and the physical processes that dominate each.

### Concepts
- **Troposphere** — The lowest layer where temperature falls with height and virtually all weather occurs.
- **Tropopause** — The boundary capping the troposphere where the temperature decline halts, limiting vertical mixing.
- **Ozone layer** — A stratospheric region of concentrated ozone that absorbs harmful ultraviolet radiation and warms the stratosphere.

nodes.name: Atmospheric composition
nodes.en: The mixture of nitrogen, oxygen, argon, and trace gases, with variable water vapor and aerosols that critically govern weather, radiation, and air quality.
nodes.cs: Směs dusíku, kyslíku, argonu a stopových plynů s proměnlivou vodní párou a aerosoly, které zásadně ovlivňují počasí, záření a kvalitu ovzduší.
nodes.brief:
The mixture of nitrogen, oxygen, argon, and trace gases, with variable water vapor and aerosols that critically govern weather, radiation, and air quality.

### Concepts
- **Permanent vs variable gases** — Gases with nearly constant proportions such as nitrogen versus those like water vapor that vary in time and place.
- **Aerosols** — Tiny suspended solid or liquid particles that scatter light and serve as nuclei for cloud droplet formation.

nodes.name: Radiation balance
nodes.en: The exchange of incoming solar shortwave and outgoing terrestrial longwave radiation whose net imbalance across latitudes drives atmospheric motion.
nodes.cs: Výměna přicházejícího slunečního krátkovlnného a odcházejícího zemského dlouhovlnného záření, jejíž čistá nerovnováha mezi zeměpisnými šířkami pohání pohyb atmosféry.
nodes.brief:
The exchange of incoming solar shortwave and outgoing terrestrial longwave radiation whose net imbalance across latitudes drives atmospheric motion.

### Concepts
- **Albedo** — The fraction of incident solar radiation reflected by a surface or the atmosphere.
- **Longwave emission** — The infrared radiation emitted by the Earth and atmosphere that carries energy back to space.

nodes.name: Atmospheric Thermodynamics
nodes.en: Governs how air changes temperature, pressure, and moisture as it rises, sinks, and mixes, setting the conditions under which clouds and storms can develop.
nodes.cs: Řídí, jak se vzduch mění v teplotě, tlaku a vlhkosti při stoupání, klesání a míšení, a stanovuje podmínky, za nichž mohou vznikat oblaka a bouře.

nodes.name: Adiabatic processes
nodes.en: Temperature changes in rising or sinking air caused purely by expansion or compression without heat exchange, quantified by the dry and moist lapse rates.
nodes.cs: Změny teploty ve stoupajícím či klesajícím vzduchu způsobené výhradně rozpínáním nebo stlačováním bez výměny tepla, vyjádřené suchoadiabatickým a nasyceně adiabatickým gradientem.
nodes.brief:
Temperature changes in rising or sinking air caused purely by expansion or compression without heat exchange, quantified by the dry and moist lapse rates.

### Concepts
- **Dry adiabatic lapse rate** — The rate of about 9.8 degrees Celsius per kilometer at which unsaturated rising air cools.
- **Moist adiabatic lapse rate** — The lower cooling rate of saturated rising air, reduced by latent heat released during condensation.

nodes.name: Atmospheric stability
nodes.en: The tendency of displaced air to return to or accelerate away from its origin, determined by comparing the environmental lapse rate to the adiabatic rate.
nodes.cs: Sklon vychýleného vzduchu vracet se k výchozí poloze nebo se od ní vzdalovat, určený porovnáním teplotního gradientu prostředí s adiabatickým gradientem.
nodes.brief:
The tendency of displaced air to return to or accelerate away from its origin, determined by comparing the environmental lapse rate to the adiabatic rate.

### Concepts
- **Stable air** — Air that resists vertical displacement and returns to its original level, suppressing cloud growth.
- **Unstable air** — Air in which a displaced parcel keeps rising, promoting convection and towering clouds.
- **Temperature inversion** — A layer where temperature increases with height, strongly suppressing vertical motion and trapping pollutants.

nodes.name: Humidity & moisture
nodes.en: The water-vapor content of air and its measures—mixing ratio, relative humidity, dew point—that determine when saturation and condensation occur.
nodes.cs: Obsah vodní páry ve vzduchu a jeho míry – směšovací poměr, relativní vlhkost, rosný bod – které určují, kdy nastává nasycení a kondenzace.
nodes.brief:
The water-vapor content of air and its measures—mixing ratio, relative humidity, dew point—that determine when saturation and condensation occur.

### Concepts
- **Relative humidity** — The ratio of actual water vapor present to the maximum the air can hold at that temperature.
- **Dew point** — The temperature to which air must be cooled at constant pressure to reach saturation.

nodes.name: Atmospheric Dynamics
nodes.en: Explains the forces and balances that set air in motion, from the pressure-gradient force to the Coriolis effect, producing the winds and circulations of the planet.
nodes.cs: Vysvětluje síly a rovnováhy, jež uvádějí vzduch do pohybu, od síly tlakového gradientu po Coriolisův jev, a vytvářejí větry a cirkulace planety.

nodes.name: Pressure gradient & geostrophic wind
nodes.en: The balance between the force driving air from high to low pressure and the deflecting Coriolis force, yielding winds that flow nearly parallel to isobars aloft.
nodes.cs: Rovnováha mezi silou hnající vzduch z vyššího do nižšího tlaku a odklánějící Coriolisovou silou, dávající vzniknout větrům proudícím ve výšce téměř rovnoběžně s izobarami.
nodes.brief:
The balance between the force driving air from high to low pressure and the deflecting Coriolis force, yielding winds that flow nearly parallel to isobars aloft.

### Concepts
- **Pressure gradient force** — The force per unit mass directed from high toward low pressure that initiates air movement.
- **Geostrophic wind** — The theoretical wind resulting from balance between the pressure gradient and Coriolis forces, blowing parallel to isobars.

nodes.name: Coriolis effect & general circulation
nodes.en: The apparent deflection of moving air by Earth's rotation, organizing the atmosphere into the trade winds, westerlies, and three circulation cells per hemisphere.
nodes.cs: Zdánlivé odklánění pohybujícího se vzduchu rotací Země, jež uspořádává atmosféru do pasátů, západních větrů a tří cirkulačních buněk na každé polokouli.
nodes.brief:
The apparent deflection of moving air by Earth's rotation, organizing the atmosphere into the trade winds, westerlies, and three circulation cells per hemisphere.

### Concepts
- **Coriolis effect** — The deflection of moving objects to the right in the Northern Hemisphere and left in the Southern due to Earth's rotation.
- **Hadley cell** — The tropical circulation loop of rising air near the equator and sinking air near thirty degrees latitude.
- **Trade winds & westerlies** — The persistent surface wind belts of the tropics and mid-latitudes generated by the circulation cells.

nodes.name: Jet streams
nodes.en: Fast, narrow ribbons of upper-level wind concentrated along strong temperature gradients, steering surface weather systems and separating air masses.
nodes.cs: Rychlé úzké pásy větru ve vyšších hladinách soustředěné podél silných teplotních gradientů, jež usměrňují přízemní tlakové útvary a oddělují vzduchové hmoty.
nodes.brief:
Fast, narrow ribbons of upper-level wind concentrated along strong temperature gradients, steering surface weather systems and separating air masses.

### Concepts
- **Polar jet stream** — A powerful upper-level wind at mid-latitudes along the boundary between polar and warmer air.
- **Rossby waves** — Large meandering north-south undulations of the jet stream that shape the progression of weather.

nodes.name: Cloud Physics & Precipitation
nodes.en: Explains how water vapor condenses onto nuclei to form cloud droplets and ice crystals, and how these grow until they fall as rain, snow, or hail.
nodes.cs: Vysvětluje, jak vodní pára kondenzuje na jádrech a vytváří oblačné kapičky a ledové krystalky a jak tyto rostou, dokud nespadnou jako déšť, sníh či kroupy.

nodes.name: Cloud formation
nodes.en: The condensation of vapor onto aerosol nuclei when rising air cools to saturation, producing suspended droplets or ice crystals that constitute clouds.
nodes.cs: Kondenzace páry na aerosolových jádrech, když se stoupající vzduch ochladí k nasycení, jež vytváří vznášející se kapičky nebo ledové krystalky tvořící oblaka.
nodes.brief:
The condensation of vapor onto aerosol nuclei when rising air cools to saturation, producing suspended droplets or ice crystals that constitute clouds.

### Concepts
- **Condensation nuclei** — Microscopic particles onto which water vapor condenses to begin forming cloud droplets.
- **Lifting mechanisms** — Processes such as convection, orographic ascent, and frontal lifting that raise air to its saturation level.

nodes.name: Precipitation mechanisms
nodes.en: The growth of cloud particles to precipitable size through collision-coalescence in warm clouds and the Bergeron process in mixed-phase clouds.
nodes.cs: Růst oblačných částic na srážkovou velikost prostřednictvím spojování při srážkách v teplých oblacích a Bergeronova procesu v oblacích smíšené fáze.
nodes.brief:
The growth of cloud particles to precipitable size through collision-coalescence in warm clouds and the Bergeron process in mixed-phase clouds.

### Concepts
- **Collision-coalescence** — The merging of falling droplets that collide, dominant in warm clouds without ice.
- **Bergeron process** — The growth of ice crystals at the expense of supercooled droplets in mixed-phase clouds, producing most mid-latitude precipitation.

nodes.name: Cloud classification
nodes.en: The systematic naming of clouds by altitude and form, from low stratus and cumulus to high cirrus, encoding the atmospheric conditions that generate them.
nodes.cs: Systematické pojmenování oblaků podle výšky a tvaru, od nízkých stratů a kumulů po vysoké cirry, jež zachycuje atmosférické podmínky, které je vytvářejí.
nodes.brief:
The systematic naming of clouds by altitude and form, from low stratus and cumulus to high cirrus, encoding the atmospheric conditions that generate them.

### Concepts
- **Cumulus** — Detached, heaped clouds with flat bases and cauliflower tops formed by convection.
- **Stratus** — Uniform layered clouds covering the sky in a featureless sheet, often producing drizzle.
- **Cirrus** — Thin, wispy high-altitude clouds composed of ice crystals.

nodes.name: Synoptic & Mesoscale Meteorology
nodes.en: Studies weather-producing systems from continental-scale fronts and cyclones down to individual thunderstorms and tornadoes, the phenomena of daily forecasts.
nodes.cs: Zkoumá útvary tvořící počasí od kontinentálních front a cyklon až po jednotlivé bouřky a tornáda – jevy každodenních předpovědí.

nodes.name: Air masses & fronts
nodes.en: Large bodies of air with uniform temperature and humidity, and the boundaries between them where contrasting air meets to trigger clouds and precipitation.
nodes.cs: Rozsáhlé objemy vzduchu s jednotnou teplotou a vlhkostí a rozhraní mezi nimi, kde se střetává kontrastní vzduch a spouští oblačnost a srážky.
nodes.brief:
Large bodies of air with uniform temperature and humidity, and the boundaries between them where contrasting air meets to trigger clouds and precipitation.

### Concepts
- **Cold front** — The leading edge of advancing cold air that lifts warm air steeply, often producing intense but brief storms.
- **Warm front** — The boundary where warm air overrides retreating cold air, bringing gradual widespread precipitation.
- **Occluded front** — A front formed when a cold front overtakes a warm front, lifting warm air off the surface.

nodes.name: Cyclones & anticyclones
nodes.en: Rotating low- and high-pressure systems that organize surface weather, with converging rising air bringing storms and diverging sinking air bringing clear skies.
nodes.cs: Rotující útvary nízkého a vysokého tlaku, jež organizují přízemní počasí; sbíhající se stoupající vzduch přináší bouře, rozbíhající se klesající vzduch jasnou oblohu.
nodes.brief:
Rotating low- and high-pressure systems that organize surface weather, with converging rising air bringing storms and diverging sinking air bringing clear skies.

### Concepts
- **Mid-latitude cyclone** — A large low-pressure system with fronts that dominates weather in temperate zones.
- **Tropical cyclone** — An intense warm-core storm fueled by ocean heat, known as a hurricane or typhoon at full strength.

nodes.name: Thunderstorms & tornadoes
nodes.en: Deep convective storms driven by instability and moisture, capable of producing lightning, hail, damaging winds, and rotating funnels that reach the ground.
nodes.cs: Hluboké konvektivní bouře poháněné instabilitou a vlhkostí, schopné vytvářet blesky, kroupy, ničivý vítr a rotující chobotnice dosahující k zemi.
nodes.brief:
Deep convective storms driven by instability and moisture, capable of producing lightning, hail, damaging winds, and rotating funnels that reach the ground.

### Concepts
- **Supercell** — A long-lived thunderstorm with a persistent rotating updraft that produces the most violent tornadoes and hail.
- **Wind shear** — The change of wind speed or direction with height that organizes and sustains severe storms.

nodes.name: Weather Forecasting
nodes.en: Predicts future atmospheric states by combining dense observations with numerical models that integrate the governing physical equations forward in time.
nodes.cs: Předpovídá budoucí stavy atmosféry spojováním hustých pozorování s numerickými modely, jež integrují řídící fyzikální rovnice dopředu v čase.

nodes.name: Numerical weather prediction
nodes.en: The computational solution of the fluid-dynamics and thermodynamics equations on a grid, advancing an initial atmospheric state to forecast its evolution.
nodes.cs: Výpočetní řešení rovnic dynamiky tekutin a termodynamiky na síti, jež posouvá počáteční stav atmosféry vpřed k předpovědi jejího vývoje.
nodes.brief:
The computational solution of the fluid-dynamics and thermodynamics equations on a grid, advancing an initial atmospheric state to forecast its evolution.

### Concepts
- **Data assimilation** — The process of blending observations into a model to produce the best estimate of the current atmospheric state.
- **Ensemble forecasting** — Running many slightly perturbed model runs to quantify forecast uncertainty.
- **Chaos & predictability limit** — The sensitive dependence on initial conditions that caps useful weather forecasts at roughly two weeks.

nodes.name: Observation & remote sensing
nodes.en: The global network of surface stations, radiosondes, radar, and satellites that measures the atmosphere and supplies the initial data models require.
nodes.cs: Globální síť přízemních stanic, radiosond, radarů a družic, jež měří atmosféru a dodává počáteční data, která modely potřebují.
nodes.brief:
The global network of surface stations, radiosondes, radar, and satellites that measures the atmosphere and supplies the initial data models require.

### Concepts
- **Weather radar** — An instrument that emits microwaves and detects their reflection off precipitation to map its location and intensity.
- **Radiosonde** — A balloon-borne instrument package that measures the vertical profile of temperature, humidity, and wind.

nodes.en: The study of the oceans as physical and chemical systems — currents, tides, waves, seawater composition, seafloor topography, and their interaction with climate. Distinct from Marine Biology (ocean life) and Hydrology (fresh water on land): its subject is the physical ocean itself.
nodes.cs: Studium oceánů jako fyzikálních a chemických systémů — proudů, přílivů, vln, složení mořské vody, topografie dna a jejich vzájemného působení s klimatem. Odlišná od mořské biologie (mořský život) a hydrologie (sladká voda na souši): jejím předmětem je fyzikální oceán samotný.
nodes.brief:
Oceanography is the study of the oceans as physical and chemical systems — currents, tides, waves, seawater composition and density, seafloor topography, and the exchanges of heat and gas between sea and sky. It treats the ocean as a single interconnected body whose circulation redistributes heat around the planet and stores carbon on timescales far longer than the atmosphere can.

Within [[01.04]], oceanography's circulation and heat storage set boundary conditions that [[01.04.02]] draws on to forecast weather and that [[01.04.04]] draws on to characterize long-term climate regimes, making it a physical bridge between the two. It borders [[01.04.09]] at the coastline: hydrology tracks fresh water on land, while oceanography takes over once that water, and the rivers carrying it, reach the sea.

For further orientation, see NOAA's [Ocean Exploration and Research](https://oceanexplorer.noaa.gov/) program and the [Encyclopaedia Britannica entry on oceanography](https://www.britannica.com/science/oceanography).

nodes.name: Physical Oceanography
nodes.en: Studies the motion and physical properties of seawater—currents, waves, tides, temperature, and salinity structure—that transport heat and mass across the global ocean.
nodes.cs: Zkoumá pohyb a fyzikální vlastnosti mořské vody – proudy, vlny, přílivy, teplotní a salinitní strukturu – jež přenášejí teplo a hmotu napříč světovým oceánem.

nodes.name: Ocean circulation
nodes.en: The system of wind-driven surface currents and density-driven deep flow that redistributes heat globally, coupling the ocean to climate over decades to millennia.
nodes.cs: Soustava větrem hnaných povrchových proudů a hustotou řízeného hlubinného proudění, jež globálně přerozděluje teplo a spojuje oceán s klimatem v měřítku desetiletí až tisíciletí.
nodes.brief:
The system of wind-driven surface currents and density-driven deep flow that redistributes heat globally, coupling the ocean to climate over decades to millennia.

### Concepts
- **Gyres** — Large rotating systems of surface currents driven by prevailing winds and shaped by the Coriolis effect.
- **Thermohaline circulation** — The deep overturning flow driven by differences in water density from temperature and salinity.
- **Ekman transport** — The net movement of wind-driven surface water at an angle to the wind due to Earth's rotation.
- **Upwelling** — The rise of cold nutrient-rich deep water to the surface where winds push surface water away.

nodes.name: Ocean waves
nodes.en: The oscillations of the sea surface generated chiefly by wind, transferring energy across ocean basins without net transport of water until they break on shore.
nodes.cs: Kmity mořské hladiny vytvářené převážně větrem, jež přenášejí energii přes oceánské pánve bez čistého transportu vody, dokud se nezlomí u břehu.
nodes.brief:
The oscillations of the sea surface generated chiefly by wind, transferring energy across ocean basins without net transport of water until they break on shore.

### Concepts
- **Wave anatomy** — The crest, trough, wavelength, and period that describe a wave's geometry and energy.
- **Swell** — Regular long-period waves that have traveled beyond the storm that generated them.
- **Tsunami** — A long-wavelength wave generated by a sudden displacement of the seafloor, such as an earthquake.

nodes.name: Tides
nodes.en: The periodic rise and fall of sea level produced by the gravitational pull of the Moon and Sun combined with Earth's rotation.
nodes.cs: Periodické stoupání a klesání hladiny moře vyvolané gravitačním působením Měsíce a Slunce ve spojení s rotací Země.
nodes.brief:
The periodic rise and fall of sea level produced by the gravitational pull of the Moon and Sun combined with Earth's rotation.

### Concepts
- **Spring & neap tides** — The maximal and minimal tidal ranges when the Sun and Moon align or oppose at right angles.
- **Tidal range** — The vertical difference between consecutive high and low water, varying with coastal geometry.

nodes.name: Chemical Oceanography
nodes.en: Examines the chemical composition of seawater and the biogeochemical cycles of elements and gases that regulate ocean fertility and the global carbon budget.
nodes.cs: Zkoumá chemické složení mořské vody a biogeochemické cykly prvků a plynů, jež řídí úrodnost oceánu a globální uhlíkovou bilanci.

nodes.name: Seawater composition & salinity
nodes.en: The dissolved salts giving seawater its near-constant ionic proportions, with salinity controlling density and thereby driving deep circulation.
nodes.cs: Rozpuštěné soli dávající mořské vodě její téměř stálé iontové poměry, přičemž salinita řídí hustotu, a tím pohání hlubinnou cirkulaci.
nodes.brief:
The dissolved salts giving seawater its near-constant ionic proportions, with salinity controlling density and thereby driving deep circulation.

### Concepts
- **Salinity** — The total mass of dissolved salts per unit mass of seawater, averaging about 35 grams per kilogram.
- **Principle of constant proportions** — The observation that the major dissolved ions occur in nearly fixed ratios regardless of total salinity.

nodes.name: Ocean carbon cycle
nodes.en: The uptake, storage, and release of carbon by the ocean through solubility and biological pumps, making the sea the largest active reservoir of atmospheric carbon.
nodes.cs: Příjem, uložení a uvolňování uhlíku oceánem prostřednictvím rozpustnostní a biologické pumpy, které z moře činí největší aktivní zásobárnu atmosférického uhlíku.
nodes.brief:
The uptake, storage, and release of carbon by the ocean through solubility and biological pumps, making the sea the largest active reservoir of atmospheric carbon.

### Concepts
- **Solubility pump** — The dissolution of carbon dioxide into cold dense surface water that then sinks, transporting carbon to depth.
- **Biological pump** — The transfer of carbon to the deep sea via sinking organic matter produced by plankton.
- **Ocean acidification** — The decline in seawater pH as absorbed carbon dioxide forms carbonic acid.

nodes.name: Nutrients & dissolved gases
nodes.en: The distribution of nitrogen, phosphorus, and silica that limits primary production, and of oxygen and carbon dioxide that structure marine chemistry with depth.
nodes.cs: Rozložení dusíku, fosforu a křemíku, jež limituje primární produkci, a kyslíku a oxidu uhličitého, jež s hloubkou utvářejí mořskou chemii.
nodes.brief:
The distribution of nitrogen, phosphorus, and silica that limits primary production, and of oxygen and carbon dioxide that structure marine chemistry with depth.

### Concepts
- **Limiting nutrient** — The essential element in shortest supply that constrains the rate of biological production.
- **Oxygen minimum zone** — A depth range where dissolved oxygen is severely depleted by respiration and weak ventilation.

nodes.name: Biological Oceanography
nodes.en: Investigates marine life and the ecological processes that link organisms to the physical and chemical ocean, from microscopic plankton to whole ecosystems.
nodes.cs: Zkoumá mořský život a ekologické procesy, jež propojují organismy s fyzikálním a chemickým oceánem, od mikroskopického planktonu po celé ekosystémy.

nodes.name: Marine primary production
nodes.en: The photosynthetic fixation of carbon by phytoplankton in the sunlit surface layer, forming the base of nearly all ocean food webs.
nodes.cs: Fotosyntetická fixace uhlíku fytoplanktonem v osvětlené povrchové vrstvě, jež tvoří základ téměř všech oceánských potravních sítí.
nodes.brief:
The photosynthetic fixation of carbon by phytoplankton in the sunlit surface layer, forming the base of nearly all ocean food webs.

### Concepts
- **Phytoplankton** — Microscopic drifting algae that perform most of the ocean's photosynthesis.
- **Photic zone** — The sunlit upper layer of the ocean where photosynthesis can occur.

nodes.name: Marine ecosystems
nodes.en: The organized communities of the coral reef, open ocean, deep sea, and polar seas, each shaped by light, temperature, and nutrient availability.
nodes.cs: Organizovaná společenstva korálových útesů, otevřeného oceánu, hlubokého moře a polárních moří, z nichž každé je utvářeno světlem, teplotou a dostupností živin.
nodes.brief:
The organized communities of the coral reef, open ocean, deep sea, and polar seas, each shaped by light, temperature, and nutrient availability.

### Concepts
- **Coral reef** — A biologically diverse structure built by colonial coral animals in warm shallow tropical seas.
- **Hydrothermal vent community** — A deep-sea ecosystem sustained by chemosynthesis around superheated mineral-rich springs.

nodes.name: Marine Geology
nodes.en: Studies the structure, sediments, and evolution of the ocean floor, revealing seafloor spreading, deep-sea deposition, and the record of past ocean conditions.
nodes.cs: Zkoumá stavbu, sedimenty a vývoj oceánského dna a odhaluje rozpínání dna, hlubokomořské usazování a záznam dřívějších oceánských podmínek.

nodes.name: Seafloor topography
nodes.en: The major features of the ocean bottom—continental margins, abyssal plains, mid-ocean ridges, and trenches—that record the tectonic life cycle of oceanic crust.
nodes.cs: Hlavní útvary oceánského dna – kontinentální okraje, abysální roviny, středooceánské hřbety a hlubokomořské příkopy – jež zaznamenávají tektonický životní cyklus oceánské kůry.
nodes.brief:
The major features of the ocean bottom—continental margins, abyssal plains, mid-ocean ridges, and trenches—that record the tectonic life cycle of oceanic crust.

### Concepts
- **Mid-ocean ridge** — An undersea mountain chain where new oceanic crust forms by seafloor spreading.
- **Ocean trench** — A deep, narrow depression marking where oceanic crust subducts into the mantle.
- **Abyssal plain** — A flat deep-ocean floor blanketed by fine sediment far from continents.

nodes.name: Marine sediments
nodes.en: The accumulating deposits of terrigenous, biogenic, and authigenic origin that blanket the seafloor and archive climatic and biological history.
nodes.cs: Hromadící se usazeniny terigenního, biogenního a autigenního původu, jež pokrývají mořské dno a uchovávají klimatické a biologické dějiny.
nodes.brief:
The accumulating deposits of terrigenous, biogenic, and authigenic origin that blanket the seafloor and archive climatic and biological history.

### Concepts
- **Biogenic ooze** — Deep-sea sediment composed mainly of the microscopic shells of plankton.
- **Turbidite** — A graded sediment deposit laid down by a dense sediment-laden current flowing down the continental slope.

nodes.name: Ocean-Atmosphere Interaction
nodes.en: Examines the coupled exchange of heat, moisture, momentum, and gases between sea and air that drives climate variability on seasonal to interannual scales.
nodes.cs: Zkoumá provázanou výměnu tepla, vlhkosti, hybnosti a plynů mezi mořem a ovzduším, jež pohání proměnlivost klimatu v měřítku od sezonního po meziroční.

nodes.name: El Niño–Southern Oscillation
nodes.en: The dominant mode of interannual climate variability, in which coupled shifts of tropical Pacific sea-surface temperature and trade winds reorganize global weather.
nodes.cs: Převládající způsob meziroční klimatické proměnlivosti, při němž provázané změny teploty povrchu tropického Pacifiku a pasátů přeskupují globální počasí.
nodes.brief:
The dominant mode of interannual climate variability, in which coupled shifts of tropical Pacific sea-surface temperature and trade winds reorganize global weather.

### Concepts
- **El Niño** — The warm phase marked by weakened trade winds and warming of the eastern tropical Pacific.
- **La Niña** — The cool phase with strengthened trade winds and cooler eastern Pacific waters.
- **Teleconnection** — A statistical linkage between climate anomalies in widely separated regions.

nodes.name: Air-sea heat & gas exchange
nodes.en: The transfer of energy through evaporation, radiation, and conduction, and of gases such as carbon dioxide and oxygen across the sea surface.
nodes.cs: Přenos energie odpařováním, zářením a vedením a plynů jako oxid uhličitý a kyslík přes mořskou hladinu.
nodes.brief:
The transfer of energy through evaporation, radiation, and conduction, and of gases such as carbon dioxide and oxygen across the sea surface.

### Concepts
- **Latent heat flux** — The energy the ocean loses to the atmosphere through evaporation of surface water.
- **Gas transfer velocity** — The rate at which a gas crosses the air-sea interface, controlled largely by wind and turbulence.

nodes.name: Coastal & Estuarine Processes
nodes.en: Studies the dynamic zone where land, sea, and rivers meet, governed by waves, tides, and sediment transport that continually reshape shorelines.
nodes.cs: Zkoumá dynamickou zónu, kde se stýkají pevnina, moře a řeky, řízenou vlnami, přílivy a transportem sedimentu, jež neustále přetváří pobřeží.

nodes.name: Coastal dynamics
nodes.en: The interplay of wave energy, longshore currents, and sediment supply that erodes and builds beaches, spits, and barrier islands.
nodes.cs: Souhra energie vln, podél pobřeží proudících proudů a přísunu sedimentu, jež erodují a budují pláže, kosy a bariérové ostrovy.
nodes.brief:
The interplay of wave energy, longshore currents, and sediment supply that erodes and builds beaches, spits, and barrier islands.

### Concepts
- **Longshore drift** — The transport of sediment along a shoreline by waves striking the coast at an angle.
- **Barrier island** — A long offshore sand ridge parallel to the coast that shields the mainland from waves.

nodes.name: Estuaries
nodes.en: Semi-enclosed coastal bodies where freshwater rivers mix with seawater, creating salinity gradients that sustain highly productive transitional ecosystems.
nodes.cs: Polouzavřené pobřežní útvary, kde se sladkovodní řeky mísí s mořskou vodou a vytvářejí salinitní gradienty udržující vysoce produktivní přechodové ekosystémy.
nodes.brief:
Semi-enclosed coastal bodies where freshwater rivers mix with seawater, creating salinity gradients that sustain highly productive transitional ecosystems.

### Concepts
- **Salt wedge** — A layer of denser seawater intruding beneath outflowing river water in a stratified estuary.
- **Estuarine circulation** — The two-layer flow of seaward surface freshwater over landward-moving bottom seawater.

nodes.en: The study of climate — the long-term statistics of atmospheric conditions, their spatial patterns, drivers, and change over years to millennia. Where Meteorology forecasts near-term weather, Climatology characterizes the enduring regimes and their evolution, including anthropogenic climate change.
nodes.cs: Studium klimatu — dlouhodobých statistik atmosférických podmínek, jejich prostorových vzorců, hnacích sil a změn v horizontu let až tisíciletí. Kde meteorologie předpovídá počasí v blízké době, klimatologie charakterizuje trvalé režimy a jejich vývoj, včetně antropogenní změny klimatu.
nodes.brief:
Climatology is the study of climate — the long-term statistics of atmospheric conditions, their spatial patterns, the forces that drive them, and how they change over years to millennia. It analyzes historical records, proxy data, and model simulations to characterize enduring regimes rather than any single day's weather, including the trajectory of anthropogenic climate change.

Within [[01.04]], climatology is distinguished from [[01.04.02]] by timescale — meteorology forecasts the atmosphere's near-term state, while climatology characterizes its long-run statistics — and it draws heavily on [[01.04.03]], whose ocean circulation and heat storage shape climate regimes over decades. It also relies on [[01.04.10]] for deep-time evidence: ice cores preserve a record of past temperature and atmospheric composition that extends climatology's view far beyond the instrumental era.

For further reading, see NASA's [climate change resource](https://climate.nasa.gov/) and the [IPCC](https://www.ipcc.ch/) assessment reports.

nodes.name: Climate System & Energy Balance
nodes.en: Treats climate as the coupled system of atmosphere, ocean, land, ice, and biosphere whose long-term state is set by the planetary radiation budget and its feedbacks.
nodes.cs: Pojímá klima jako provázaný systém atmosféry, oceánu, pevniny, ledu a biosféry, jehož dlouhodobý stav určuje planetární zářivá bilance a její zpětné vazby.

nodes.name: Global energy budget
nodes.en: The long-term balance between absorbed solar radiation and emitted terrestrial radiation that fixes Earth's mean temperature.
nodes.cs: Dlouhodobá rovnováha mezi pohlceným slunečním zářením a vyzářeným zemským zářením, jež stanovuje střední teplotu Země.
nodes.brief:
The long-term balance between absorbed solar radiation and emitted terrestrial radiation that fixes Earth's mean temperature.

### Concepts
- **Radiative equilibrium** — The steady state in which outgoing longwave radiation matches absorbed solar radiation.
- **Latitudinal energy imbalance** — The surplus of energy in the tropics and deficit at the poles that drives heat transport toward higher latitudes.

nodes.name: Greenhouse effect
nodes.en: The warming produced when atmospheric gases absorb and re-emit terrestrial infrared radiation, raising surface temperature above the radiative-equilibrium value.
nodes.cs: Oteplení vznikající, když atmosférické plyny pohlcují a znovu vyzařují zemské infračervené záření, čímž zvyšují teplotu povrchu nad hodnotu zářivé rovnováhy.
nodes.brief:
The warming produced when atmospheric gases absorb and re-emit terrestrial infrared radiation, raising surface temperature above the radiative-equilibrium value.

### Concepts
- **Greenhouse gases** — Gases such as water vapor, carbon dioxide, and methane that absorb infrared radiation and warm the atmosphere.
- **Radiative forcing** — A change in the energy balance measured in watts per square meter caused by an external factor.

nodes.name: Feedback mechanisms
nodes.en: The internal responses that amplify or dampen an initial climate change, such as ice-albedo and water-vapor feedbacks that determine climate sensitivity.
nodes.cs: Vnitřní odezvy, jež zesilují nebo tlumí počáteční klimatickou změnu, jako je zpětná vazba led-albedo a vodní páry, které určují klimatickou citlivost.
nodes.brief:
The internal responses that amplify or dampen an initial climate change, such as ice-albedo and water-vapor feedbacks that determine climate sensitivity.

### Concepts
- **Ice-albedo feedback** — A positive loop in which melting bright ice exposes dark surfaces that absorb more heat and drive further melting.
- **Water-vapor feedback** — The amplification of warming as a warmer atmosphere holds more water vapor, itself a greenhouse gas.
- **Climate sensitivity** — The equilibrium warming expected from a doubling of atmospheric carbon dioxide.

nodes.name: Climate Classification & Controls
nodes.en: Organizes the world's climates into coherent types and identifies the geographic factors—latitude, elevation, ocean currents, continentality—that determine them.
nodes.cs: Uspořádává klimata světa do soudržných typů a určuje zeměpisné činitele – zeměpisnou šířku, nadmořskou výšku, mořské proudy, kontinentalitu – které je určují.

nodes.name: Köppen classification
nodes.en: The widely used scheme that groups climates by temperature and precipitation thresholds tied to characteristic vegetation, from tropical to polar.
nodes.cs: Široce užívaný systém, jenž třídí klimata podle prahů teploty a srážek vázaných na charakteristickou vegetaci, od tropického po polární.
nodes.brief:
The widely used scheme that groups climates by temperature and precipitation thresholds tied to characteristic vegetation, from tropical to polar.

### Concepts
- **Climate zones** — The five broad groups—tropical, dry, temperate, continental, and polar—of the classification.
- **Climograph** — A graph combining monthly temperature and precipitation used to characterize and compare climates.

nodes.name: Climate controls
nodes.en: The physical factors governing regional climate, including latitude, altitude, distance from the sea, prevailing winds, and ocean currents.
nodes.cs: Fyzikální činitele řídící regionální klima, včetně zeměpisné šířky, nadmořské výšky, vzdálenosti od moře, převládajících větrů a mořských proudů.
nodes.brief:
The physical factors governing regional climate, including latitude, altitude, distance from the sea, prevailing winds, and ocean currents.

### Concepts
- **Continentality** — The greater temperature range of inland regions compared with the moderating influence of the sea.
- **Rain shadow** — The dry region on the leeward side of a mountain range where descending air suppresses precipitation.

nodes.name: Paleoclimatology
nodes.en: Reconstructs climates of the geologic past from natural archives, revealing the range of natural variability and the mechanisms behind glacial cycles.
nodes.cs: Rekonstruuje klimata geologické minulosti z přírodních archivů a odhaluje rozsah přirozené proměnlivosti a mechanismy stojící za ledovcovými cykly.

nodes.name: Climate proxies
nodes.en: Indirect indicators such as ice cores, tree rings, and sediment isotopes that record past temperature, precipitation, and atmospheric composition.
nodes.cs: Nepřímé ukazatele jako ledovcové vrty, letokruhy a izotopy v sedimentech, jež zaznamenávají dřívější teplotu, srážky a složení atmosféry.
nodes.brief:
Indirect indicators such as ice cores, tree rings, and sediment isotopes that record past temperature, precipitation, and atmospheric composition.

### Concepts
- **Ice core** — A cylinder of glacial ice whose trapped air bubbles and layering preserve a continuous climate record.
- **Oxygen isotope ratio** — The ratio of oxygen-18 to oxygen-16 in shells or ice used to infer past temperature and ice volume.
- **Dendroclimatology** — The reconstruction of past climate from the width and density of annual tree rings.

nodes.name: Ice ages & Milankovitch cycles
nodes.en: The alternation of glacial and interglacial periods paced by cyclic variations in Earth's orbit that modulate the seasonal distribution of sunlight.
nodes.cs: Střídání dob ledových a meziledových udávané cyklickými změnami oběžné dráhy Země, jež modulují sezonní rozložení slunečního záření.
nodes.brief:
The alternation of glacial and interglacial periods paced by cyclic variations in Earth's orbit that modulate the seasonal distribution of sunlight.

### Concepts
- **Eccentricity, obliquity, precession** — The three orbital parameters whose cyclic variation alters insolation and paces glacial cycles.
- **Glacial-interglacial cycle** — The roughly 100,000-year alternation between extensive ice sheets and warmer interglacial conditions.

nodes.name: Climate Change & Anthropogenic Forcing
nodes.en: Analyzes the ongoing warming driven by human greenhouse-gas emissions, using observations and models to attribute change and project future scenarios.
nodes.cs: Analyzuje probíhající oteplování hnané lidskými emisemi skleníkových plynů a pomocí pozorování a modelů přisuzuje změnu a projektuje budoucí scénáře.

nodes.name: Greenhouse gas forcing
nodes.en: The positive radiative forcing from rising concentrations of carbon dioxide, methane, and nitrous oxide released by fossil fuels, agriculture, and land use.
nodes.cs: Kladné zářivé působení z rostoucích koncentrací oxidu uhličitého, metanu a oxidu dusného uvolňovaných fosilními palivy, zemědělstvím a využíváním půdy.
nodes.brief:
The positive radiative forcing from rising concentrations of carbon dioxide, methane, and nitrous oxide released by fossil fuels, agriculture, and land use.

### Concepts
- **Keeling curve** — The continuous record of rising atmospheric carbon dioxide measured since 1958 at Mauna Loa.
- **Carbon budget** — The cumulative emissions consistent with limiting warming to a chosen temperature target.

nodes.name: Climate modeling
nodes.en: The use of coupled numerical models simulating atmosphere, ocean, land, and ice to project climate under specified emission pathways.
nodes.cs: Užití provázaných numerických modelů simulujících atmosféru, oceán, pevninu a led k projekci klimatu za daných emisních scénářů.
nodes.brief:
The use of coupled numerical models simulating atmosphere, ocean, land, and ice to project climate under specified emission pathways.

### Concepts
- **General circulation model** — A three-dimensional model solving the physical equations of the climate system on a global grid.
- **Emission scenario** — A plausible future trajectory of greenhouse-gas emissions used as input to climate projections.

nodes.name: Regional & Applied Climatology
nodes.en: Applies climate science at local scales, characterizing microclimates, urban effects, and recurring variability patterns relevant to society and planning.
nodes.cs: Uplatňuje klimatologii v lokálním měřítku, charakterizuje mikroklima, městské vlivy a opakující se způsoby proměnlivosti významné pro společnost a plánování.

nodes.name: Microclimates & urban climate
nodes.en: The local modification of climate by surface cover, topography, and cities, most visibly the warming of built-up areas relative to their surroundings.
nodes.cs: Lokální pozměnění klimatu povrchovým krytem, terénem a městy, nejnápadněji oteplení zastavěných ploch oproti jejich okolí.
nodes.brief:
The local modification of climate by surface cover, topography, and cities, most visibly the warming of built-up areas relative to their surroundings.

### Concepts
- **Urban heat island** — The elevated temperature of a city compared with its rural surroundings caused by dark surfaces and waste heat.
- **Microclimate** — The distinctive climate of a small area shaped by local surface and topographic conditions.

nodes.name: Climate variability modes
nodes.en: Recurring large-scale patterns such as the North Atlantic Oscillation and monsoons that reorganize regional weather over months to years.
nodes.cs: Opakující se rozsáhlé vzory jako Severoatlantická oscilace a monzuny, jež přeskupují regionální počasí v měřítku měsíců až let.
nodes.brief:
Recurring large-scale patterns such as the North Atlantic Oscillation and monsoons that reorganize regional weather over months to years.

### Concepts
- **North Atlantic Oscillation** — A seesaw in atmospheric pressure between Iceland and the Azores that steers European winter weather.
- **Monsoon** — A seasonal reversal of prevailing winds that brings pronounced wet and dry seasons, driven by land-sea heating contrasts.

nodes.en: The study of ancient life through fossils — the identification, classification, and interpretation of preserved organisms and traces to reconstruct evolutionary history and past environments. It bridges Earth Sciences and Biology, distinct from Geology's focus on rocks and processes by centering on the record of life itself.
nodes.cs: Studium dávného života prostřednictvím zkamenělin — určování, klasifikace a výklad zachovaných organismů a stop k rekonstrukci evoluční historie a dávných prostředí. Propojuje vědy o Zemi a biologii; od geologie zaměřené na horniny a procesy se liší důrazem na samotný záznam života.
nodes.brief:
Paleontology is the study of ancient life through fossils — the identification, classification, and interpretation of preserved organisms and traces to reconstruct evolutionary history and past environments. It bridges the natural-history disciplines, reading the deep past from physical remains rather than direct observation.

Within [[01.04]], paleontology depends on [[01.04.01]] for the stratigraphic context that dates its fossils and situates them within the rock record, while its subject matter — living organisms across deep time — connects it directly to [[01.03]], whose evolutionary theory paleontology's fossil record tests and extends. It shares its deep-time perspective with [[01.04.10]]: where paleontology reads Earth's history in fossils, glaciology reads a complementary record in ice cores.

For further orientation, see the Smithsonian's [Department of Paleobiology](https://naturalhistory.si.edu/research/paleobiology) and the [UC Museum of Paleontology's Understanding Evolution](https://evolution.berkeley.edu/) resource.

nodes.name: Taphonomy & Fossilization
nodes.en: Studies how organisms decay, are buried, and become fossils, and the biases this imposes on the fossil record's completeness and interpretation.
nodes.cs: Zkoumá, jak organismy rozkládají, jak jsou pohřbívány a jak se stávají zkamenělinami, a jaké zkreslení to vnáší do úplnosti a výkladu fosilního záznamu.

nodes.name: Fossilization processes
nodes.en: The physical and chemical pathways—burial, mineral replacement, and lithification—by which organic remains are preserved in rock over geologic time.
nodes.cs: Fyzikální a chemické cesty – pohřbení, nahrazení minerály a zpevnění – jimiž se organické zbytky uchovávají v hornině po geologicky dlouhou dobu.
nodes.brief:
The physical and chemical pathways—burial, mineral replacement, and lithification—by which organic remains are preserved in rock over geologic time.

### Concepts
- **Permineralization** — The filling of pore spaces in tissue by dissolved minerals that harden, preserving fine internal structure.
- **Molds & casts** — The impression left by a dissolved organism and the replica formed when that void later fills with sediment.

nodes.name: Types of preservation
nodes.en: The spectrum of fossil quality from common hard-part remains to rare soft-tissue and trace fossils that expand our view of ancient life.
nodes.cs: Škála kvality zkamenělin od běžných zbytků tvrdých částí po vzácné zkameněliny měkkých tkání a stopové zkameněliny, jež rozšiřují náš pohled na dávný život.
nodes.brief:
The spectrum of fossil quality from common hard-part remains to rare soft-tissue and trace fossils that expand our view of ancient life.

### Concepts
- **Lagerstätte** — A rare fossil deposit of exceptional preservation, often including soft tissues.
- **Trace fossil** — A preserved record of activity such as tracks, burrows, or coprolites rather than the organism's body.

nodes.name: Invertebrate Paleontology
nodes.en: Documents the fossil record of animals without backbones, which dominate the marine record and provide the workhorse fossils for dating rock strata.
nodes.cs: Dokumentuje fosilní záznam bezobratlých živočichů, kteří převládají v mořském záznamu a poskytují klíčové zkameněliny pro datování horninových vrstev.

nodes.name: Trilobites & arthropods
nodes.en: The extinct segmented arthropods that flourished throughout the Paleozoic, serving as index fossils and revealing early animal diversification.
nodes.cs: Vyhynulí členovci se segmentovaným tělem, kteří vzkvétali po celý paleozoikum a slouží jako vůdčí zkameněliny a odhalují raný rozvoj živočichů.
nodes.brief:
The extinct segmented arthropods that flourished throughout the Paleozoic, serving as index fossils and revealing early animal diversification.

### Concepts
- **Trilobite** — A three-lobed marine arthropod, one of the most abundant and diverse fossils of the Paleozoic era.
- **Exoskeleton molting** — The periodic shedding of the hard outer covering that produces many arthropod fossils.

nodes.name: Molluscs & brachiopods
nodes.en: Shelled invertebrates whose durable calcium-carbonate skeletons make them among the most common and stratigraphically useful marine fossils.
nodes.cs: Ulitnatí a lasturnatí bezobratlí, jejichž odolné vápenaté schránky z nich činí jedny z nejběžnějších a stratigraficky nejužitečnějších mořských zkamenělin.
nodes.brief:
Shelled invertebrates whose durable calcium-carbonate skeletons make them among the most common and stratigraphically useful marine fossils.

### Concepts
- **Ammonite** — An extinct coiled cephalopod whose rapidly evolving shell forms are prime index fossils of the Mesozoic.
- **Brachiopod** — A bivalved marine animal, superficially clam-like but distinct, abundant in Paleozoic seas.

nodes.name: Vertebrate Paleontology
nodes.en: Traces the fossil history of backboned animals from early fishes through the rise of tetrapods, dinosaurs, and mammals, charting major evolutionary transitions.
nodes.cs: Sleduje fosilní dějiny obratlovců od raných ryb přes vzestup čtvernožců, dinosaurů a savců a mapuje velké evoluční přechody.

nodes.name: Fishes & tetrapod origins
nodes.en: The evolution of vertebrates from jawless fishes to jawed fishes and the fin-to-limb transition that brought vertebrate life onto land.
nodes.cs: Vývoj obratlovců od bezčelistnatých ryb k čelistnatým rybám a přechod od ploutve k končetině, jenž přivedl obratlovce na souš.
nodes.brief:
The evolution of vertebrates from jawless fishes to jawed fishes and the fin-to-limb transition that brought vertebrate life onto land.

### Concepts
- **Sarcopterygian** — A lobe-finned fish lineage whose sturdy fins foreshadow the limbs of land vertebrates.
- **Tiktaalik** — A transitional fossil bridging fish and early tetrapods with both gills and weight-bearing fins.

nodes.name: Dinosaurs & Mesozoic reptiles
nodes.en: The dominant terrestrial and marine reptiles of the Mesozoic, whose diversity, physiology, and bird ancestry are central problems of the discipline.
nodes.cs: Převládající suchozemští a mořští plazi druhohor, jejichž rozmanitost, fyziologie a ptačí předci jsou ústředními otázkami oboru.
nodes.brief:
The dominant terrestrial and marine reptiles of the Mesozoic, whose diversity, physiology, and bird ancestry are central problems of the discipline.

### Concepts
- **Dinosauria** — A clade of archosaurian reptiles that dominated terrestrial ecosystems for over 160 million years.
- **Bird origins** — The evolutionary derivation of birds from small feathered theropod dinosaurs.
- **K–Pg extinction** — The asteroid-linked mass extinction that ended the non-avian dinosaurs 66 million years ago.

nodes.name: Mammalian evolution
nodes.en: The rise of mammals from synapsid ancestors and their Cenozoic diversification into the dominant large-bodied land animals after the dinosaurs' demise.
nodes.cs: Vzestup savců ze synapsidních předků a jejich kenozoické rozrůznění v převládající velké suchozemské živočichy po zániku dinosaurů.
nodes.brief:
The rise of mammals from synapsid ancestors and their Cenozoic diversification into the dominant large-bodied land animals after the dinosaurs' demise.

### Concepts
- **Synapsid** — The amniote lineage, including mammal ancestors, defined by a single opening behind each eye socket.
- **Cenozoic radiation** — The rapid diversification of mammals into vacant ecological roles after the end-Cretaceous extinction.

nodes.name: Paleobotany & Micropaleontology
nodes.en: Studies fossil plants and microscopic organisms, which record the greening of the continents and provide the finest-scale tools for dating and environmental reconstruction.
nodes.cs: Zkoumá fosilní rostliny a mikroskopické organismy, jež zaznamenávají ozelenění kontinentů a poskytují nejjemnější nástroje pro datování a rekonstrukci prostředí.

nodes.name: Fossil plants
nodes.en: The record of terrestrial vegetation from early land plants through coal-forming forests to flowering plants, tracking the colonization and greening of land.
nodes.cs: Záznam suchozemské vegetace od raných suchozemských rostlin přes uhlotvorné lesy po krytosemenné rostliny, jenž sleduje osídlení a ozelenění souše.
nodes.brief:
The record of terrestrial vegetation from early land plants through coal-forming forests to flowering plants, tracking the colonization and greening of land.

### Concepts
- **Carboniferous coal forests** — Vast swampy forests whose accumulated plant matter formed most of the world's coal.
- **Angiosperm radiation** — The rapid mid-Cretaceous diversification of flowering plants that transformed terrestrial ecosystems.

nodes.name: Microfossils
nodes.en: The abundant microscopic remains of plankton and pollen that enable high-resolution biostratigraphy and paleoenvironmental analysis of sediment cores.
nodes.cs: Hojné mikroskopické zbytky planktonu a pylu, jež umožňují vysoce rozlišenou biostratigrafii a paleoenvironmentální analýzu sedimentových jader.
nodes.brief:
The abundant microscopic remains of plankton and pollen that enable high-resolution biostratigraphy and paleoenvironmental analysis of sediment cores.

### Concepts
- **Foraminifera** — Single-celled marine protists with calcite shells, key microfossils for dating and paleoclimate.
- **Palynology** — The study of fossil pollen and spores used to reconstruct past vegetation and climate.

nodes.name: Evolutionary Paleobiology & Biostratigraphy
nodes.en: Interprets the fossil record to understand evolutionary patterns, mass extinctions, and the use of fossil successions to correlate and date rock strata worldwide.
nodes.cs: Vykládá fosilní záznam k pochopení evolučních vzorů, hromadných vymírání a využití sledu zkamenělin ke korelaci a datování horninových vrstev po celém světě.

nodes.name: Mass extinctions
nodes.en: The episodes of rapid, widespread loss of biodiversity that punctuate the fossil record and reset the course of evolution, notably the Big Five.
nodes.cs: Epizody rychlého plošného úbytku biodiverzity, jež přerušují fosilní záznam a přesměrovávají chod evoluce, zejména takzvaných Velkých pět.
nodes.brief:
The episodes of rapid, widespread loss of biodiversity that punctuate the fossil record and reset the course of evolution, notably the Big Five.

### Concepts
- **Permian-Triassic extinction** — The most severe extinction in Earth history, eliminating the majority of marine species.
- **Background vs mass extinction** — The distinction between the steady baseline rate of species loss and catastrophic global pulses.

nodes.name: Biostratigraphy
nodes.en: The correlation and relative dating of rock strata by their contained fossil assemblages, using rapidly evolving, widespread species as time markers.
nodes.cs: Korelace a relativní datování horninových vrstev podle obsažených společenstev zkamenělin s využitím rychle se vyvíjejících rozšířených druhů jako časových značek.
nodes.brief:
The correlation and relative dating of rock strata by their contained fossil assemblages, using rapidly evolving, widespread species as time markers.

### Concepts
- **Index fossil** — A widespread, short-lived species used to identify and correlate the age of the rock containing it.
- **Faunal succession** — The principle that fossil organisms follow one another through strata in a definite, recognizable order.

nodes.name: Evolutionary patterns
nodes.en: The macroevolutionary tempo and mode read from fossils, including debates over gradual versus punctuated change and adaptive radiations.
nodes.cs: Makroevoluční tempo a způsob čtený ze zkamenělin, včetně sporů o postupnou versus přerušovanou změnu a adaptivní radiace.
nodes.brief:
The macroevolutionary tempo and mode read from fossils, including debates over gradual versus punctuated change and adaptive radiations.

### Concepts
- **Punctuated equilibrium** — The model that species remain stable for long spans then change rapidly during brief speciation events.
- **Adaptive radiation** — The rapid diversification of a lineage into many forms exploiting new ecological opportunities.

nodes.en: The study of minerals — their chemical composition, crystal structure, physical properties, formation, and classification.
